description: Credit card bust-out scheme — rapid maxing of credit line, no payment made, account closure triggered
parsed_text:
SUSPICIOUS ACTIVITY REPORT
SAR ID: SAR-2024-02903
Filing Institution: Trident Consumer Credit Union
Filing Date: 2024-11-01
Subject: Kevin Thomas Drummond   Tax ID: XXX-XX-8817
Subject Address: 1102 Sycamore Hill Court, Charlotte, NC 28205
Account: XXXX5506
Activity Dates: 2024-08-01 to 2024-09-15
Suspicious Activity Type: Fraud
Amount Involved: USD 42,800.00   Transaction Count: 38
Law Enforcement Contacted: No
Branch: Charlotte Eastside Branch
Investigator: Brenda Fitzgerald

Narrative: Trident Consumer Credit Union is filing this Suspicious Activity Report regarding a credit card bust-out fraud scheme executed through the account of member Kevin Thomas Drummond (date of birth: 04/28/1990). Mr. Drummond has been a credit union member since 2022 and maintained a credit card account with a credit limit of $42,800.

For approximately eighteen months prior to August 2024, Mr. Drummond maintained the account in good standing with regular monthly payments ranging from $200 to $500, keeping an average utilization ratio below 25%. Beginning on August 1, 2024, the account's spending pattern changed materially. Over the 46-day period ending September 15, 2024, Mr. Drummond made thirty-eight (38) transactions totaling $42,800, effectively exhausting his entire available credit line. Transaction categories included electronics retailers ($14,200), gift card purchases at grocery and pharmacy locations ($11,900), a single jewelry purchase ($6,400), hotel reservations ($5,300), and general merchandise ($5,000).

No payment was received on the account after July's scheduled minimum payment. The August statement balance of $18,600 went unpaid. The September statement cycle closed with the account fully utilized at $42,800. A payment due notice generated on September 22, 2024 went unanswered. Attempts to contact Mr. Drummond by phone, email, and mailed correspondence produced no response. A review of the address on file for Mr. Drummond revealed that the property is a rental unit listed as vacant since September 1, 2024, on public utility records.

The gift card purchases are a particular concern as they represent a commonly identified cash-out mechanism in credit card fraud. The geographic spread of transactions across six states over the 46-day period further suggests coordinated bust-out activity. The account has been charged off. No prior SARs are on file for Mr. Drummond.

expected_json.narrative_summary: A credit union member with 18 months of responsible payment history rapidly exhausted a $42,800 credit line over 46 days through gift card purchases, electronics, and scattered multi-state transactions, then became completely uncontactable and vacated his known address, resulting in a charge-off. The pattern is consistent with a deliberate credit card bust-out fraud scheme.
